description: Shared Renovate preset for jr200 / jr200-labs / whengas. Extend from a repo's renovate.json with: { "extends": ["github>jr200/github-action-templates"] }

packageRules.description: Group every non-major bump into a single PR per repo. Schedule deliberately omitted so the wave lands immediately; grouping prevents PR-spam without needing a time window. No automerge — humans review every PR.
Major upgrades always require explicit human review
Group all GitHub Action version bumps together
Group all Docker base image bumps together

Don't bump the Python runtime constraint — that's a deliberate stack decision and not something Renovate should touch. Also sidesteps the 'pep440: failed to calculate newValue' warning Renovate hits when trying to bump '>=3.12,<3.14' style ranges.

Disable Renovate's built-in helm-values manager. We use a custom regex manager (above) that maps ghcr.io image refs to the github-tags datasource instead, which avoids the per-package access list ghcr.io enforces for private packages. Letting both managers detect the same files would create duplicate, conflicting bump attempts (the helm-values one fails with 'Failed to look up docker package ... no-result' on every run).

customManagers.description: Bump private git-pinned Python deps in pep621 pyproject.toml — e.g. 'pyapi-service-kit[nats,db,otel] @ git+https://github.com/whengas/pyapi-service-kit.git@v0.7.3'

Bump container image tags in whengas-iac Helm values.yaml files — e.g. 'tag: v0.1.27' under 'repository: ghcr.io/whengas/whengas-api'. Uses the github-tags datasource (not docker), because all our ghcr.io images are pushed from corresponding github repos with matching git tags, and the App token has cross-repo contents:read but not always packages:read on each individual private package. The capture extracts the org/repo path from the ghcr.io reference and queries github tags directly, which sidesteps the per-package access list ghcr.io enforces for private packages.
